title: Latent tire-state features from a lightweight state-space degradation filter
summary: Recent F1 work models tire wear as a latent state with pit-stop resets and shows better predictive robustness than simple autoregressive baselines.
rationale: Recent F1 work models tire wear as a latent state with pit-stop resets and shows better predictive robustness than simple autoregressive baselines. Your current features proxy degradation directly, but not the hidden pace state or uncertainty around it.
implementation_hint: Per `(Year,Race,Driver,Stint)`, fit a simple online filter/state-space approximation on prior laps only: latent pace level, degradation slope, one-step residual, and uncertainty. Feed these filtered states into the existing tabular model. Start with a lightweight Kalman-style approximation rather than full Bayesian inference.
expected_effect: Adds a more causal degradation signal than raw `Cumulative_Degradation` and rolling means, potentially helping across compounds and noisy laps.
risk: Implementation complexity is higher than standard feature engineering, and badly specified filters may just restate existing rolling features.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
